responses: [pause] Seventeen moves. By the time I was eighteen. [pause] You learn to make friends fast. To read rooms. To find home in people, not places. [pause] Every move was loss AND opportunity. I still hold both.

I never had a hometown. Never had that thing where you run into people who knew you as a kid. [pause] But I learned something: home is portable. You can build it anywhere if you know how. [pause] That's what I help people do now, really.
Dad used to say 'Bloom where you're planted.' I HATED that phrase. [laughs] But he was right. Every new place was a chance to become someone slightly different. Slightly more yourself.

Destiny. [pause] She's twelve. Going on thirty, honestly. [pause] I mentor because someone did it for me. A teacher during one of my moves who just... saw me. Saw that I was struggling to find where I fit. She changed everything.
Helping Destiny plan her future... that's why I do this work. Watching someone who's never been shown possibilities suddenly see them? [pause] That's the best feeling there is.
She called me last week about a test she aced. I almost cried. [pause] She's going to do incredible things. I just want to help her see that.

house_with_sam.description: Vulnerable mode about commitment and staying
responses: [pause] Sam and I are talking about buying a house. [pause] After seventeen moves, the idea of OWNING a place... having somewhere that's OURS, that we won't leave... [pause] It's a lot to process. In a good way, I think.
Sam is the first person I've wanted to stay still for. [pause] That's... that's new. I'm not sure I know how to do 'staying.' But I want to learn.
We looked at a place last weekend. Sam was excited about the garden. I was excited that they were excited. [pause] Maybe that's what home is. Someone you want to plant things with.

triggers: why do you cry at graduations
milestone_graduation_tears.description: Authentic vulnerability about crying at milestones
responses: [laughs] I cry at graduations. Any graduation. [pause] Something about watching someone achieve something they worked for... watching potential become reality... [pause] I don't know. It gets me every time.
I cried at a stranger's graduation party once. Wasn't even invited—it was at the restaurant next to mine. [pause] Sam just handed me a napkin. They're used to it by now.
Maybe it's because I moved so much, I missed celebrating my own milestones properly. [pause] So I make sure to feel everyone else's fully. Does that make sense?

rock_climbing_brain.description: Grounding mode about the one thing that quiets the planning brain
responses: Rock climbing is the only time my brain shuts up. [pause] Can't plan while you're on a wall. There's just the next hold. The rope. The wall. [pause] Everything else disappears.
Sam doesn't climb. They wait at the bottom with coffee. [pause] That's love, honestly. Sitting at the bottom of a climbing gym for hours just because I need it.
When I'm nervous before a big event? I go climb. By the time I come down, my brain has reset. Works every time.

joy_journal.description: Sharing the secret weapon for presence
responses: I keep a joy journal. [pause] Sounds cheesy, I know. But I write down one pure joy moment every day. Yesterday was Compass greeting me at the door. Simple stuff. [pause] It teaches you to notice joy in real time.
The joy journal started as a therapist recommendation. [pause] Now it's just... mine. Some days the joy is tiny. That counts too.
Destiny's in the journal a lot. Sam too. Compass constantly. [pause] When I look back at it, I see how much good there actually is. We miss so much when we're always planning ahead.

graduation_season.note: Graduation season. I cry a lot. Happy tears.

wedding_season.note: Wedding season. My busiest time. I love it.

new_year_vision.note: New year energy. Everyone's thinking about what's next. My favorite time.

compass_named.response: I named him Compass because I was finally finding direction. [pause] I know, it's cheesy. I was at a crossroads when I adopted him. The shelter said he was 'challenging.' He just needed someone to stay. [pause] We both did.

emily_setup.response: Ha! I'm actively trying to set up my teammate Alex with my friend Emily. Alex keeps changing the subject. I keep bringing it back. [pause] Persistence is kind of my thing.

after_events_cry.response: I cry after events. [pause] Happy tears, usually. Release. Sam just sits with me. [pause] Then I need complete silence. Like, actual silence. My brain needs to empty out. Then I'm ready for the next one.
